perturbation.original_text: Either party may terminate this Agreement without cause at any time effective upon thirty (30) days' written notice. Notwithstanding anything to the contrary contained in this Agreement, no termination of this Agreement for any reason whatsoever shall relieve the Customer of the obligation to pay all amounts due to i-on and to make such payments on a timely basis.
perturbation.changed_text: Either party may terminate this Agreement without cause at any time effective upon sixty (60) days' written notice. Notwithstanding anything to the contrary contained in this Agreement, no termination of this Agreement for any reason whatsoever shall relieve the Customer of the obligation to pay all amounts due to i-on and to make such payments on a timely basis.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation changes the termination notice period from thirty (30) days to sixty (60) days. This creates an in-text contradiction by altering a key term related to contract termination, specifically the amount of advance notice required. This impacts the termination terms, the obligations of the parties during this time, and it causes uncertainty for contractual obligations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The term of this Agreement for the Hosted Site shall commence upon April 1, 1999 and shall continue for a period of twelve (12) months, unless earlier terminated in accordance with provisions hereof. This Agreement shall automatically be renewed for one (1) or more one (1) month periods unless either the Customer or i-on gives notice to the other party of its intention not to renew the 4 Agreement, which notice must be given not less than fifteen (15) days before the end of the respective initial or renewal term. Either party may terminate this Agreement without cause at any time effective upon sixty (60) days' written notice. Notwithstanding anything to the contrary contained in this Agreement, no termination of this Agreement for any reason whatsoever shall relieve the Customer of the obligation to pay all amounts due to i-on and to make such payments on a timely basis.
perturbation.justification: The changed text alters the termination notice period from thirty (30) days to sixty (60) days. The contradicted text contains the exact same clause in a different section, therefore the change made in the changed text directly contradicts the contradicted text because it contains the old information, thus creating a strong in-text contradiction.
